methods.method_name: Protein Biomass and Test Size Measurements
methods.description: This method describes the process of cleaning, storing, and analyzing foraminifera individuals to measure protein biomass and test size. The procedure includes cleaning the samples, storing them, and conducting morphometric analyses to determine protein-to-size relations.

steps.description: After picking, individuals were immediately cleaned with a brush and filtered seawater to remove all particles, including organic matter, that were stuck to the test.
steps.searchable_summary: clean foraminifera samples using brush and filtered seawater

steps.description: The individuals were stored in a 1.5 mL Eppendorf vial and analysed on board, using the bicinchoninic acid (BCA) method as explained in Meilland et al., (2016).
steps.searchable_summary: store samples in Eppendorf vial and analyze using BCA method

steps.description: Morphometric analyses on single foraminiferal tests were carried out at the University of Angers with an automated incident light microscope at a resolution of 1.4 μm².
steps.searchable_summary: perform morphometric analysis using automated microscope

steps.description: Images were analysed for their two-dimensional (silhouette) morphometry, including minimum test diameter.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze images for 2D morphometry and minimum test diameter

steps.description: Protein-to-size relations were determined for the minimum diameter of each test providing size-normalized protein content (SNP) for data analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: determine protein-to-size relations for size-normalized protein content

steps.description: Foraminifera protein concentrations were linearly normalized to 1 μm minimum test diameter, being aware of any unavoidable errors related to nonlinear increments of biomass at volumetric test growth.
steps.searchable_summary: linearly normalize protein concentrations to 1 μm minimum test diameter
